reforms.description: The budget proposal for 1976/77 includes an extension of the stockbuilding scheme to the end of 1976 for firms that had not applied for subsidies by the end of 1975, as well as a scheme introduced in July 1975 to stimulate stockbuilding, allowing firms to receive compensation equivalent to 20 percent of the rise in stocks, provided employment was maintained.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms incentivize firms to maintain employment and invest in stockbuilding, which can enhance productivity and economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The stockbuilding scheme was operational and extended as part of the budget proposal for 1976/77.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly extend existing support mechanisms for firms, indicating a notable but not transformative change.
reforms.source_quote: Additional proposals contained in the budget for 1976/77 include an extension of the stockbuilding scheme to the end of 1976 for firms which had not applied for subsidies by the end of 1975, extension of the deadline for use of investment funds, and higher subsidies for manpower training in the firms.

reforms.description: In early 1975, the authorities allowed enterprises undertaking building investments to draw on ordinary investment funds throughout the year, which was later extended to all types of investments including inventory formation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform facilitates greater access to investment funds, encouraging firms to invest in various types of capital, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was enacted in early 1975 and allowed for immediate access to investment funds.
reforms.importance_rationale: The extension of access to investment funds represents a significant adjustment to support business investment.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities decided that enterprises undertaking building investments should be allowed to draw on the ordinary investment funds throughout the year and on the special investment fund for the second half of the year.
